definitions.usage_example: I saw them making out in the car.
definitions.alternative_example: I saw them kissing passionately in the car.
definitions.usage_example_es: Los vi besándose en el auto.

definitions.usage_example: It's too dark. I can't make out the license plate.
definitions.alternative_example: It's too dark. I can't discern the license plate.
definitions.usage_example_es: Está muy oscuro. No puedo distinguir la matrícula.

definitions.usage_example: What do you make of this email?
definitions.alternative_example: How do you interpret this email?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Qué entiendes de este email? / ¿Qué opinas?

definitions.usage_example: When the rain started, we made for the house.
definitions.alternative_example: When the rain started, we went towards the house.
definitions.usage_example_es: Cuando empezó la lluvia, nos dirigimos a la casa.

definitions.usage_example: Can you pick up that pencil for me?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you lift that pencil for me?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes levantar ese lápiz por mí?

definitions.usage_example: I need to pick up my kids from school.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to collect my kids from school.
definitions.usage_example_es: Tengo que recoger a mis hijos del colegio.

definitions.usage_example: The phone is ringing. Please pick it up.
definitions.alternative_example: The phone is ringing. Please answer it.
definitions.usage_example_es: El teléfono está sonando. Por favor, contéstalo.

definitions.usage_example: You need to pick up your room.
definitions.alternative_example: You need to tidy your room.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitas ordenar tu cuarto.

definitions.usage_example: I picked up some Spanish in Medellín.
definitions.alternative_example: I learned some Spanish (casually) in Medellín.
definitions.usage_example_es: Aprendí algo de español en Medellín.

definitions.usage_example: I need to pick out a gift for my mom.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to choose a gift for my mom.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito elegir un regalo para mi mamá.

definitions.usage_example: He always picks on his little brother.
definitions.alternative_example: He always teases his little brother.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él siempre se mete con su hermano pequeño.

definitions.usage_example: She is just picking at her food.
definitions.alternative_example: She is eating her food without appetite.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ella solo está picoteando su comida.

definitions.usage_example: Put on your jacket. It's cold outside.
definitions.alternative_example: Wear your jacket. It's cold outside.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ponte la chaqueta. Hace frío afuera.

definitions.usage_example: Can you put on some music?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you play some music?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes poner algo de música?

definitions.usage_example: He put on 10 pounds over the holidays.
definitions.alternative_example: He gained 10 pounds over the holidays.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él ganó 10 libras (kilos) durante las vacaciones.

definitions.usage_example: She wasn't really sad. She just put it on.
definitions.alternative_example: She wasn't really sad. She just pretended.
definitions.usage_example_es: No estaba realmente triste. Solo lo fingió.

definitions.usage_example: Never put off until tomorrow what you can do today.
definitions.alternative_example: Never postpone until tomorrow what you can do today.
definitions.usage_example_es: Nunca dejes para mañana lo que puedes hacer hoy.

definitions.usage_example: The bad smell put me off the food.
definitions.alternative_example: The bad smell discouraged me from eating the food.
definitions.usage_example_es: El mal olor me quitó las ganas de comer.

definitions.usage_example: The firefighters put out the fire quickly.
definitions.alternative_example: The firefighters extinguished the fire quickly.
definitions.usage_example_es: Los bomberos apagaron el fuego rápidamente.

definitions.usage_example: Don't forget to put out the trash.
definitions.alternative_example: Don't forget to take the trash outside.
definitions.usage_example_es: No olvides sacar la basura.

definitions.usage_example: I can't put up with his excuses anymore.
definitions.alternative_example: I can't tolerate his excuses anymore.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ya no puedo soportar sus excusas.

definitions.usage_example: Please put away your toys now.
definitions.alternative_example: Please store your toys in their proper place.
definitions.usage_example_es: Por favor, guarda tus juguetes ahora.

definitions.usage_example: You can put down your bag here.
definitions.alternative_example: You can place your bag here.
definitions.usage_example_es: Puedes dejar tu bolso aquí.

definitions.usage_example: He always puts down his coworkers.
definitions.alternative_example: He always insults his coworkers.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él siempre menosprecia a sus compañeros de trabajo.

definitions.usage_example: We had to put our old cat down.
definitions.alternative_example: We had to euthanize our old cat.
definitions.usage_example_es: Tuvimos que sacrificar a nuestro viejo gato.

definitions.usage_example: We ran out of milk this morning.
definitions.alternative_example: We exhausted our supply of milk this morning.
definitions.usage_example_es: Nos quedamos sin leche esta mañana.

definitions.usage_example: I ran into my old teacher at the store.
definitions.alternative_example: I met my old teacher unexpectedly at the store.
definitions.usage_example_es: Me encontré con mi antiguo profesor en la tienda.

definitions.usage_example: He ran into a tree with his bike.
definitions.alternative_example: He collided with a tree with his bike.
definitions.usage_example_es: Chocó contra un árbol con su bicicleta.

definitions.usage_example: A bus ran over his bicycle.
definitions.alternative_example: A bus hit his bicycle.
definitions.usage_example_es: Un autobús atropelló su bicicleta.

definitions.usage_example: The meeting ran over by 10 minutes.
definitions.alternative_example: The meeting exceeded the time by 10 minutes.
definitions.usage_example_es: La reunión se pasó 10 minutos.

definitions.usage_example: The dog ran away, but we found him.
definitions.alternative_example: The dog escaped, but we found him.
definitions.usage_example_es: El perro se escapó, pero lo encontramos.

definitions.usage_example: Let's run through the plan one more time.
definitions.alternative_example: Let's review the plan one more time.
definitions.usage_example_es: Repasemos el plan una vez más.

definitions.usage_example: Can I run this idea by you quickly?
definitions.alternative_example: Can I tell you this idea for your opinion?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedo consultarte esta idea rápidamente?

definitions.usage_example: We need to set up the chairs for the meeting.
definitions.alternative_example: We need to arrange the chairs for the meeting.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitamos montar (colocar) las sillas para la reunión.

definitions.usage_example: He said he was innocent and the police set him up.
definitions.alternative_example: He said he was innocent and the police framed him.
definitions.usage_example_es: Dijo que era inocente y que la policía le tendió una trampa.

definitions.meaning: Irse de viaje, ponerse en camino
definitions.usage_example: We set off early in the morning.
definitions.alternative_example: We started our journey early in the morning.
definitions.usage_example_es: Salimos (de viaje) temprano en la mañana.

definitions.usage_example: The smoke set off the fire alarm.
definitions.alternative_example: The smoke activated the fire alarm.
definitions.usage_example_es: El humo activó la alarma de incendios.

definitions.usage_example: The bad weather set back the construction.
definitions.alternative_example: The bad weather delayed the construction.
definitions.usage_example_es: El mal tiempo retrasó la construcción.

definitions.usage_example: How much did that new car set you back?
definitions.alternative_example: How much did that new car cost you?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Cuánto te costó ese auto nuevo?

definitions.usage_example: The rain has set in for the weekend.
definitions.alternative_example: The rain has started and will likely continue.
definitions.usage_example_es: La lluvia se ha instalado para (todo) el fin de semana.

definitions.usage_example: I set aside some money every month.
definitions.alternative_example: I save some money every month.
definitions.usage_example_es: Aparto (ahorro) algo de dinero todos los meses.

definitions.usage_example: Let's set aside our differences and work together.
definitions.alternative_example: Let's ignore our differences and work together.
definitions.usage_example_es: Dejemos a un lado nuestras diferencias y trabajemos juntos.

definitions.usage_example: He didn't show up for work today.
definitions.alternative_example: He didn't arrive at work today.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él no apareció (no vino) a trabajar hoy.

definitions.usage_example: You showed me up in front of my boss!
definitions.alternative_example: You embarrassed me in front of my boss!
definitions.usage_example_es: ¡Me hiciste quedar mal (me humillaste) frente a mi jefe!

definitions.usage_example: He's just showing off his new car.
definitions.alternative_example: He's just boasting about his new car.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él solo está presumiendo su auto nuevo.

definitions.usage_example: Let me show you around the new office.
definitions.alternative_example: Let me give you a tour of the new office.
definitions.usage_example_es: Déjame mostrarte la nueva oficina (hacerte un tour).

definitions.usage_example: Please take off your shoes at the door.
definitions.alternative_example: Please remove your shoes at the door.
definitions.usage_example_es: Por favor, quítate los zapatos en la puerta.

definitions.usage_example: What time does the plane take off?
definitions.alternative_example: What time does the plane depart/leave?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿A qué hora despega el avión?

definitions.usage_example: He saw the police and took off.
definitions.alternative_example: He saw the police and ran away.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él vio a la policía y se fue (huyó).

definitions.usage_example: It's your turn to take out the trash.
definitions.alternative_example: It's your turn to remove the trash.
definitions.usage_example_es: Es tu turno de sacar la basura.

definitions.usage_example: He's taking me out for dinner on Friday.
definitions.alternative_example: He's inviting me to dinner on Friday.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él me va a llevar a cenar el viernes.

definitions.usage_example: Let's just get take out tonight.
definitions.alternative_example: Let's just get food to go tonight.
definitions.usage_example_es: Simplemente pidamos comida para llevar esta noche.

definitions.usage_example: I'm thinking of taking up yoga.
definitions.alternative_example: I'm thinking of starting yoga.
definitions.usage_example_es: Estoy pensando en empezar a hacer yoga.

definitions.usage_example: This new sofa takes up a lot of space.
definitions.alternative_example: This new sofa occupies a lot of space.
definitions.usage_example_es: Este nuevo sofá ocupa mucho espacio.

definitions.meaning: Asumir el control, tomar el relevo
definitions.usage_example: She will take over the company next year.
definitions.alternative_example: She will assume control of the company next year.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ella asumirá el control de la empresa el próximo año.

definitions.usage_example: You really take after your father.
definitions.alternative_example: You really resemble your father.
definitions.usage_example_es: Realmente te pareces a tu padre.

definitions.usage_example: I take back what I said. I was wrong.
definitions.alternative_example: I retract what I said. I was wrong.
definitions.usage_example_es: Retiro lo dicho. Estaba equivocado.

definitions.usage_example: I need to take back these shoes. They don't fit.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to return these shoes. They don't fit.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito devolver estos zapatos. No me quedan.

definitions.usage_example: Can you take down my phone number?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you write down my phone number?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes anotar mi número de teléfono?

definitions.usage_example: It's time to take down the Christmas lights.
definitions.alternative_example: It's time to remove the Christmas lights.
definitions.usage_example_es: Es hora de quitar las luces de Navidad.

definitions.usage_example: Can you turn on the light, please?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you activate the light, please?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes encender la luz, por favor?

definitions.usage_example: That kind of confidence really turns me on.
definitions.alternative_example: That kind of confidence really attracts me.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ese tipo de confianza realmente me atrae (excita).

definitions.usage_example: Don't forget to turn off the TV.
definitions.alternative_example: Don't forget to deactivate the TV.
definitions.usage_example_es: No olvides apagar la televisión.

definitions.usage_example: His bad attitude really turns me off.
definitions.alternative_example: His bad attitude really repels me.
definitions.usage_example_es: Su mala actitud realmente me repele (desagrada).

definitions.usage_example: Can you turn up the music? I love this song.
definitions.alternative_example: Can you increase the volume of the music?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes subir la música? Me encanta esta canción.

definitions.usage_example: He finally turned up at the party at midnight.
definitions.alternative_example: He finally arrived at the party at midnight.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él finalmente apareció en la fiesta a medianoche.

definitions.usage_example: Could you turn down the TV? It's too loud.
definitions.alternative_example: Could you decrease the volume of the TV?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Podrías bajar la TV? Está muy alta.

definitions.usage_example: She turned down the job offer.
definitions.alternative_example: She rejected the job offer.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ella rechazó la oferta de trabajo.

definitions.usage_example: The caterpillar turned into a butterfly.
definitions.alternative_example: The caterpillar became a butterfly.
definitions.usage_example_es: La oruga se convirtió en mariposa.

definitions.usage_example: The movie turned out to be really good.
definitions.alternative_example: The movie was surprisingly really good.
definitions.usage_example_es: La película resultó ser muy buena.

definitions.usage_example: A lot of people turned out for the concert.
definitions.alternative_example: A lot of people attended the concert.
definitions.usage_example_es: Mucha gente acudió al concierto.

definitions.usage_example: Turn around and let me see your back.
definitions.alternative_example: Rotate and let me see your back.
definitions.usage_example_es: Date la vuelta y déjame ver tu espalda.

definitions.usage_example: What time do you usually wake up?
definitions.alternative_example: What time do you usually stop sleeping?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿A qué hora te despiertas usualmente?

definitions.usage_example: Can you wake me up at 7 AM?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you rouse me at 7 AM?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Me puedes despertar a las 7 AM?

definitions.usage_example: You need to wake up and see the problem.
definitions.alternative_example: You need to realize and see the problem.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitas espabilar (despertar) y ver el problema.

definitions.usage_example: I try to work out three times a week.
definitions.alternative_example: I try to exercise three times a week.
definitions.usage_example_es: Intento hacer ejercicio tres veces por semana.

definitions.usage_example: We need to work out a solution.
definitions.alternative_example: We need to find a solution.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitamos encontrar (resolver) una solución.

definitions.usage_example: I hope everything works out for you.
definitions.alternative_example: I hope everything goes well for you.
definitions.usage_example_es: Espero que todo te salga bien.

definitions.usage_example: What are you working on right now?
definitions.alternative_example: What project are you doing right now?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿En qué estás trabajando ahora mismo?

definitions.usage_example: I need to work on my pronunciation.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to improve my pronunciation.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito trabajar en (mejorar) mi pronunciación.

definitions.usage_example: The feature is broken, but we can work around it.
definitions.alternative_example: The feature is broken, but we can find an alternative solution.
definitions.usage_example_es: La función está rota, pero podemos encontrar una alternativa.

definitions.usage_example: We worked up a big appetite after the hike.
definitions.alternative_example: We developed a big appetite after the hike.
definitions.usage_example_es: Nos dio mucha hambre después de la caminata.
